{"query":"von der Leyen Nathalie Loiseau criticism Kaja Kallas foreign policy authority Gulf Iran MEP statement 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1721,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/09/ursula-von-der-leyen-eu-foreign-policy-more-realistic-interest-driven","title":"Von der Leyen calls for EU foreign policy to be ‘more realistic and interest-driven’ | European Union | The Guardian","content":"A prominent MEP and former French minister, Nathalie Loiseau, last week chided von der Leyen for her telephone diplomacy to Gulf leaders after the US and Israeli strikes on Iran, accusing the commission president of usurping the role of the EU foreign policy chief, Kaja Kallas.
Notably in the early days of the war, von der Leyen said “a credible transition in Iran” was urgently needed, going further than Kallas.
On Monday von der Leyen avoided criticism of the US and Israel for starting the war, saying the debate about whether the conflict was “a war of choice or a war of necessity” was a debate that “partly misses the point”.
Von der Leyen and the European Council president, António Costa, held talks on Monday via video link with leaders and senior ministers from Armenia, Bahrain, Egypt, Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Syria, Turkey and the United Arab Emirates in what Brussels billed as a manifestation of solidarity.
In an EU statement after those talks von der Leyen and Costa “expressed openness” to enhance the maritime defensive operations Aspides and Atalanta, which are aimed at protecting waterways and preventing disruption to supply chains in and around the Red Sea."},{"url":"https://www.politico.eu/article/ursula-von-der-leyen-blowback-diplomatic-overreach-european-commission/","title":"Ursula von der Leyen faces blowback over diplomatic ‘overreach’ – POLITICO","content":"BRUSSELS ― European governments are irritated over what they see as Ursula von der Leyen’s move to position herself as the EU’s chief representative abroad, saying that during the opening days of the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran she went beyond her mandate.
In the first days of the crisis, von der Leyen signaled support for regime change in Tehran and held no fewer than a dozen calls with EU and Gulf state leaders.
“Between the HRVP [High Representative Kallas], the Commission and the Council presidency, there is a risk of cacophony."},{"url":"https://www.euronews.com/my-europe/2026/03/09/von-der-leyen-and-kallas-call-on-europe-to-adapt-to-chaotic-coercive-world-order","title":"Von der Leyen and Kallas call on Europe to adapt to chaotic, coercive world order | Euronews","content":""Europe can no longer be a custodian for the old world order, for a world that has gone and will not return," Ursula von der Leyen said on Monday."},{"url":"https://www.rferl.org/a/eu-iran-statement-foreign-policy-waning-influence/33692139.html","title":"EU Statement On Iran Shows Its Diminished Role As Foreign Policy Player","content":"The European Union's 27 member states issued a joint statement expressing "utmost concern" after the bloc's foreign ministers met online for nearly three hours to discuss the American-Israeli attack on Iran over the weekend and Tehran's subsequent military response on several countries in the region.
The European Union has already imposed all kinds of sanctions on Iran, including the recent designation of IRGC as a terrorist organization.
Foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas floated the idea of enlarging the bloc's naval mission, Aspides.
German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said it wasn't the time "to lecture our partners and allies" while on the other side of the spectrum, Spanish premier Pedro Sanchez condemned the US-Israeli strikes and insisted the EU statement should mention "full respect for international law."
In parallel, Hungary and Slovakia used the Iran discussion and the prospect of higher oil prices to push for a resumption of oil flowing through the Druzhba pipeline."}]}

Europe can “no longer be a custodian for the old-world order” and needs “a more realistic and interest-driven foreign policy”, the head of the European Commission has said. Speaking to an audience of EU ambassadors on Monday, Ursula von der Leyen said the union “will always defend and uphold the rules-based system” but could no longer rely on it to defend European interests and shelter the continent from threats. A prominent MEP and former French minister, Nathalie Loiseau, last week chided von der Leyen for her telephone diplomacy to Gulf leaders after the US and Israeli strikes on Iran, accusing the commission president of usurping the role of the EU foreign policy chief, Kaja Kallas. On Monday von der Leyen avoided criticism of the US and Israel for starting the war, saying the debate about whether the conflict was “a war of choice or a war of necessity” was a debate that “partly misses the point”.","score":0.8029425,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.politico.eu/article/ursula-von-der-leyen-blowback-diplomatic-overreach-european-commission","title":"Ursula von der Leyen faces blowback over diplomatic 'overreach'","content":"Discomfort with von der Leyen's foreign policy activity has led to barely concealed tensions with Kaja Kallas. ... Kaja Kallas Nathalie Loiseau","score":0.61140573,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://caliber.az/en/post/eu-diplomats-criticise-von-der-leyen-for-diplomatic-overreach-in-iran-crisis","title":"EU diplomats criticise von der Leyen for diplomatic overreach in Iran crisis","content":"One key complaint was that von der Leyen appeared to take over the role of EU foreign service chief Kaia Kallas, responsible for coordinating","score":0.5363378,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/11/stunned-sidelined-and-disunited-how-war-in-the-middle-east-paralysed-the-eu","title":"‘Stunned, sidelined and disunited’: how war in the Middle East paralysed the EU | World news | The Guardian","content":"‘A tacit acknowledgment of the illegality of this war’ … Ursula von der Leyen. # ‘Stunned, sidelined and disunited’: how war in the Middle East paralysed the EU. “Europe can no longer be a custodian for the old-world order” and needs a “more realistic and interest-driven foreign policy”. In a major foreign policy speech this week, the European Commission president said the EU would always “defend and uphold the rules-based system” but in a precarious and chaotic world, that could no longer be relied upon. Amid the criticism, von der Leyen offered a more full-throated defence of the rules-based order – and her approach – to MEPs on Wednesday: “Seeing the world as it is, in no way diminishes our determination to fight for the world as we want it.”. It was, he said, “a tacit acknowledgment of the illegality of this war [on Iran] and Europe’s failure and unwillingness to call it out based on a perception that we need to keep Trump happy”.","score":0.5148644,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://x.com/POLITICOEurope/status/2028758610042327222?lang=en","title":"The EU's reaction to U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran has exposed ...","content":"Ursula von der Leyen and Kaja Kallas clashing over who should lead the bloc's response to the crisis.","score":0.37291673,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.43,"request_id":"0e89cdf0-9577-4ae4-a6f2-ec442936f9e7"}
